name: Steeple Langford
overview: Steeple Langford is a village and civil parish in the Wylye Valley, Wiltshire, about 6 miles north-west of Wilton and 10 miles from Salisbury, with a population of around 515 (2011 census). The largest of 'The Langfords', its parish also includes the hamlets of Hanging Langford, Little Langford and the former settlement of Bathampton. It lies on the north bank of the River Wylye within the Cranborne Chase National Landscape, and is known for its thatched cottages and the lakes created from worked-out gravel pits, now the Langford Lakes Nature Reserve.
character: A quintessential, vibrant Wylye Valley village of thatched cottages beside lakes and chalk stream, with a notably active community calendar for its size.
amenities: Rainbow on the Lake pub (c.1680, with Wylye Valley/lake views)
Langford Lakes Nature Reserve (Wiltshire Wildlife Trust)
Volunteer-run sports and social club with bar, clubhouse and cricket pavilion
Active community: film nights, live music, exercise classes, the monthly Langford Big Breakfast

schools.name: Primary schools in nearby Wylye Valley villages/Wilton

prices.summary: A sought-after Wylye Valley village; thatched and period cottages plus occasional new builds, with values supported by the lakes, chalk stream and National Landscape setting. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Birdwatching and walking at Langford Lakes Nature Reserve
Trout fishing on the River Wylye
Walks to the Iron Age hillforts of Yarnbury and Grovely Castle
Community events and the Langford Big Breakfast
placesToEat: Pubs in nearby Wylye, Stapleford and Great Wishford
pros: Beautiful chalk-stream and lakeside setting in the Cranborne Chase National Landscape
Characterful village pub with lake views
Exceptionally active community for its size
Nature reserve and excellent walking on the doorstep
cons: No village shop or school within Steeple Langford itself
No railway station; rely on Salisbury
Bypassed by but close to the busy A36
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a picturesque, community-rich Wylye Valley village with lakes and walking, who are happy to travel for shops and schools.
